summary: The papers cover a range of technical approaches to small UAS challenges, from detection and tracking to security and counter-UAS methods. One focuses on visual cooperative tracking for open-path gas sensing, combining Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy with gas tomography to map emissions like CO2 or methane. Another presents a multi-physics simulation framework for designing high-power microwave systems to neutralize autonomous small drones without kinetic or RF jamming. A third introduces a lightweight neural network architecture, YolovN-CBi, optimized for real-time detection of small, fast-moving UAVs despite their low visual contrast. A fourth addresses the resolution-speed trade-off in UAV tracking on embedded platforms by proposing a heterogeneous observer-follower framework that preserves small target features in high-resolution imagery. Finally, a cryptographic framework called Diamond is described for securing telemetry from resource-constrained IoT devices, including small drones, under adversarial wireless conditions.
